commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Good. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart pharmacies in Savannah area. Publix pharmacies offer select free generics (antibiotics, lisinopril, metformin). Mail-order through Part D plans available.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or Medicare Advantage with drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions with tiered copays. Generic drugs $0-15/month, preferred brands $30-50/month, specialty drugs 25-33% coinsurance up to $2,000 annual cap.

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month and cover annual exam + $100-200 frame/lens allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine eye exams. Savannah has adequate optometry coverage. Ophthalmology at St. Joseph's/Candler or Memorial Health for specialist referrals (2-4 weeks).
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. One pair of eyeglasses or contacts after cataract surgery.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-45/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental of Georgia, Humana) typically cover 100% preventive, 50-80% basic, 50% major. Annual maximums $1,000-2,000.
dental.dentistAvailability: Adequate in Savannah. Fewer providers than larger metros but wait times still reasonable (1-3 weeks). Some dental practices cater to seniors and accept discount plans.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Southern restaurants often have lunch specials. AARP discounts at chains.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$30 + 7% tax + 20% tip = ~$38/meal, 2x/week = ~$310/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Xfinity and AT&T serve Savannah area.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: Savannah has many free parks and squares.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: Chatham Area Transit (CAT) monthly pass. Seniors 65+ ride for half-fare. Free DOT downtown shuttle.

publicTransit.coverage: Limited. CAT bus covers main Savannah corridors but frequency is low (30-60 min headways). Free DOT (Downtown) shuttle loops through historic district. Very car-dependent outside downtown.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ get half-fare on all CAT routes with Medicare card. Free DOT shuttle available to all.

rideShare.availability: Moderate. Uber and Lyft available in Savannah area but fewer drivers than major metros. Wait times can be 5-15 minutes.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available. Chatham County Senior Citizens Inc. offers subsidized transportation for seniors. Taxi services also available.

registration.notes: Georgia registration $20/year. Title fee $18. No personal property tax on vehicles in Georgia (unlike Virginia). Ad valorem tax (TAVT) is one-time at purchase (6.6% of value).

insurance.notes: Georgia car insurance rates near national average. Savannah rates moderate. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%).

fuel.notes: ~$3.20/gallon average in Savannah. GA gas tax $0.312/gallon. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year. Lower fuel costs than Northeast.

parking.notes: Free parking in most residential and suburban areas. Historic district metered parking $1.25/hour. Monthly parking passes for historic district $85-130.

tolls.notes: Minimal tolls in Savannah area. GA 400 toll removed. Occasional bridge tolls on I-95 corridor for travel to SC. Peach Pass for managed lanes on I-16 corridor.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Georgia Mature Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). Chatham County Senior Citizens offers door-to-door transport for qualifying seniors.

transportation.walkability: High in historic district. Savannah's grid layout with 22 squares makes the historic core very walkable. Outside the historic district, car-dependent. Tybee Island requires car or bus.

cycling.infrastructure: Flat terrain is bike-friendly. Some bike lanes in historic district and Ardsley Park. Savannah is one of the more bikeable small cities in the South. No formal bike share program.
cycling.notes: Flat city with mild winters makes cycling viable 9-10 months/year. Heat limits summer cycling. Bike rental shops available.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: Georgia Tax Breaks
discounts.description: Georgia exempts up to $65,000 of retirement income per person (65+) from state income tax. Social Security partially exempt. Georgia income tax rate 1-5.49%.

discounts.name: CAT Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Half-price bus fares for riders 65+. Present Medicare card as proof. Free DOT downtown shuttle for everyone.

discounts.name: Chatham County Senior Services
discounts.description: Senior Citizens Inc. offers meals, transportation, activities, and social services. Multiple senior center locations in Chatham County.

discounts.name: Georgia Homestead Exemption
discounts.description: Standard $2,000 homestead exemption. Additional exemptions for seniors 62+ and 65+ that can exempt school taxes and additional county taxes based on income.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year). Fort Pulaski National Monument nearby.

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. GA wireless tax ~12% + federal USF. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: Georgia has no residential rental tax. Chatham County property tax millage rate ~34 mills — landlord responsibility. Georgia offers homestead exemptions but only for owner-occupied properties.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. No statutory limit on security deposit in Georgia. Typical deposit is 1-2 months rent.
housing.marketNotes: Historic district 2BR houses command premium ($2,000+). Better value in Midtown, Ardsley Park, and Thunderbolt. Hurricane insurance adds to landlord costs reflected in rent.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Savannah has a strong Black community with many experienced salons, particularly along MLK Jr. Blvd and the Southside.

providers.name: The Hair Gallery Savannah
providers.notes: Natural hair, locs, color, weaves, braids. Black-owned. Walk-ins welcome.

providers.name: Kinks & Curls Natural Hair Salon
providers.notes: Specializes in natural hair care, TWA to waist-length. Color services, deep conditioning.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Historic district and Southside have good salon options. Lower prices than major metros.

providers.name: Civetta Salon
providers.notes: Highly rated for color and highlights. Experienced with grey coverage and blending.

providers.name: Fantastic Sams / Great Clips
providers.notes: Budget-friendly cuts and basic color. Senior discount available.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: Limited big & tall brick-and-mortar in Savannah. DXL in nearby Jacksonville (2 hrs). Online shopping recommended.

providers.name: Belk (Oglethorpe Mall)
providers.notes: Extended sizes section. Southern brands. Good for dress shirts and khakis.

providers.name: Walmart / Target
providers.notes: George and Goodfellow brands go up to 3XL/44 waist. Budget-friendly basics.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Oglethorpe Mall and Tanger Outlets (Pooler) provide good shopping. Broughton Street downtown has boutiques.

providers.name: Belk (Oglethorpe Mall)
providers.notes: Full women's department. Regular sizes well-stocked. Alterations available.

providers.name: Tanger Outlets (Pooler)
providers.notes: J.Crew, Banana Republic, LOFT outlets. 30-70% off retail. Great for stocking up.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: DSW at the outlet mall, Belk carries comfort brands. Rack Room Shoes is a good local option.

providers.name: DSW (Pooler)
providers.notes: Wide widths available. Good selection of comfort and dress shoes.

providers.name: Rack Room Shoes
providers.notes: Mid-range prices. Athletic, casual, dress. Extended sizes in some styles.
